item.title: Qatar-Exxon LNG Plant Marks First Production From Texas Facility
item.description: The Texas joint venture between QatarEnergy and Exxon Mobil Corp. has started liquefied natural gas production from the first of its three trains at the facility, marking a major milestone for US exports of the fuel as global supplies dwindle.

item.title: Citadel Securities Sees Bonds Return as Haven Amid Growth Risks
item.description: Bonds are reasserting themselves as a hedge against risk as investors worried about growing tensions in the Middle East shift their focus from inflation to slowing growth, said Citadel Securities.

item.title: US Natural Gas Drops on Warmer Outlook, Seasonal Storage Shift
item.description: US natural gas futures fell on forecasts showing warmer weather in the eastern US than previously expected, as well as market expectations that domestic stockpiles will increase relative to seasonally normal levels in the coming weeks as demand lowers moving into spring.

item.title: Biren Tech’s Revenue Triples on China’s Demand for AI Chips
item.description: Shanghai Biren Technology Co.’s annual revenue more than tripled, driven by China’s surging demand for artificial intelligence chips.

item.title: Fannie, Freddie Soar as Ackman Calls ‘Stupidly Cheap’
item.description: Shares of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac are clawing back part of a months-long decline after investor Bill Ackman called the mortgage finance giants “stupidly cheap.”

item.title: Nasdaq Speeds Up Index Entry for SpaceX, Large IPOs With New Rule
item.description: Nasdaq Inc. will enact a rule change designed to slash the time it takes for newly listed, large-cap companies to enter its main index, a move that will give shares of behemoths like SpaceX a faster route into funds that are pegged to the benchmark.

item.title: FirstRand Names Lytania Johnson FNB CEO in Leadership Shakeup
item.description: FirstRand Ltd. restructured its business and announced leadership changes as Africa’s biggest lender by market value seeks to position itself as South Africa’s most-profitable retail and commercial bank.

item.title: Saylor’s Strategy Takes Break After 13 Consecutive Weeks of Bitcoin Buying
item.description: Just days after unveiling plans to raise $42 billion to bolster its purchases of Bitcoin, Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. took a pause after 13 consecutive announced weekly acquisitions of the cryptocurrency.

item.title: UBS Joins Morgan Stanley in Touting Hedges Across Emerging World
item.description: UBS AG is telling investors to seek protection against potential losses in emerging-market credit as the fallout from the escalating war in Iran threatens developing economies.

item.title: Fed’s Powell to Speak at Harvard Economics Class
item.description: Chairman of the US Federal Reserve Jerome Powell Photographer: Al Drago/Bloomberg

item.title: Uber Buys Chauffeur Booking App Blacklane as It Courts High-End Riders
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. has acquired the chauffeur booking app Blacklane, the company’s latest move to court a high-end clientele.

item.title: Iran War Fuel-Price Shock Is Catching Up With African Nations
item.description: The fuel-supply shock is starting to hit Africa, weeks after Asian nations reported shortfalls in stocks.

item.title: Israel Holds Rates as Iran War Shows Little Sign of Easing
item.description: Israel kept interest rates unchanged, taking a cautious approach as the Iran war fuels global and domestic inflation concerns.

item.title: SEC Semiannual Reporting Plan Advances to White House for Review
item.description: The White House is reviewing a proposal from the Securities and Exchange Commission tied to semiannual corporate disclosures, a key hurdle that signals the agency is close to making the measure public.

item.title: Philippines Boosts Fuel Stocks as It Looks to Americas for Supply
item.description: The Philippines said it increased its stockpile of petroleum products to 51 days, as the import-dependent nation searches the world for alternative suppliers while the war in Iran continues.

item.title: Brazil Economists Lift Inflation Bets Through 2028 as Oil Surges
item.description: Brazil economists lifted their inflation forecasts through 2028 further above target as the war in Iran war pushes up global fuel prices and prompts investors to scale back bets on monetary easing.

item.title: Tankers Carrying Diesel Toward Europe Change Course in Atlantic
item.description: Tankers that were carrying diesel toward Europe have switched course in the Atlantic, another sign of the global tug-of-war for increasingly tight fuels as the Iran war disrupts supply chains.

item.title: Air Canada CEO to Quit After Furor About Crash Condolence Video
item.description: Air Canada Chief Executive Officer Michael Rousseau announced he’s leaving after creating a public relations fiasco with an English-only video statement about the deadly runway collision at LaGuardia Airport in New York.

item.title: Mozambique Overtakes Senegal as Africa’s Most Distressed Issuer
item.description: A surge in Mozambique’s sovereign yield premia versus Treasuries has seen it overtake Senegal to become the most distressed sovereign issuer in Africa.

item.title: German Inflation Surges to Highest in More Than Year on War
item.description: German inflation accelerated sharply in March after the Iran war boosted energy costs, supporting the idea that the European Central Bank will have to raise interest rates.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Alcoa, Spire, Sysco, Viridian Therapeutics
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.6% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York. US and Israeli forces pressed ahead with attacks on Iran while the Islamic Republic launched missiles across the Persian Gulf, sending oil prices higher once again.

item.title: Credit Market Sours for Energy-Dependent Europe as War Grinds On
item.description: Exposure to energy imports is separating winners from losers in the global credit market, with Europe turning to a major pain point as the Iran war shows few signs of ending soon.

item.title: US Stock Futures Rise as Oversold Signals Lure Some Dip Buyers
item.description: Contracts tied to US stocks climbed on Monday, putting a pause on a war-triggered selloff that spurred the longest stretch of losses since 2022 as investors and strategists across Wall Street suggested the selling was getting overdone.

item.title: Meta Faces $310 Billion Market Value Drop on Legal, AI Concerns
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. was looking like the best Big Tech stock in the market when the year began. But investors’ fears of legal risks and heavy spending on artificial intelligence are bubbling to the surface, culminating in last week’s 11% rout.

item.title: Apple Fined £390,000 by UK Over Russian Sanction Breaches
item.description: Apple Inc.’s European subsidiary was fined £390,000 ($516,110) by the UK for breaching Russian sanctions.

item.title: Oil Shipping Rates Surge as US Barrels Replace Middle East Supply
item.description: Bookings signal rising US crude flows while vessels remain stuck in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Big Tech Stocks Rout Is Flashing Signals of a Turnaround
item.description: The wreckage in large technology stocks that sent the Nasdaq 100 Index into a correction is flashing signs that have marked turning points for the group in the past.

item.title: Egypt’s President Appeals to Trump to Bring Iran War to an End
item.description: Egyptian President Abdel-Fattah El-Sisi appealed to his US counterpart Donald Trump to find a way to end to an Iran war that’s sent global energy prices soaring and threatens developing nations’ economies.

item.title: OpenAI’s ChatGPT App Store Took Aim at Apple, But Results Lag So Far
item.description: Six months in, ChatGPT’s third-party apps offer limited functionality and have been frustrating for developers.

item.title: Dubai Slumps, Muscat Soars as Gulf Stocks Diverge on War
item.description: Gulf stock markets have diverged sharply since the Iran war began, and the region is now home to both the best-performing global bourse over the past month — and the worst.
